``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined California’s prison system is dominated by determinate sentences and parolable sentences with long minimum terms. Prison sentences are generally expressed as “determinate terms” that operate as judicial maximum sentences.
## In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year In 2020, there were 1,959 people in prison past their parole eligibility year. This group made up 4% of people in prison for new crimes and with sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Demographics The demographics of people in prison past their parole eligibility year reveal notable proportions among Hispanic, any race and white, non-hispanic people. Gender distribution indicates a predominance of males over females. Age-wise, the majority of people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Criminal Offenses ::: {.cell} ::: In 2020, 69% of people in prison past their parole eligibility year were in prison for violent offenses. The breakdown of criminal offenses of people in prison past their parole eligibility year reveals a varied landscape, with the majority of people incarcerated for aggravated or simple assault (25%) and robbery (20%) offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Original Sentence Lengths In 2020, among the prison population eligible for parole but not yet released, people with sentences between 5 to 9.9 years constituted the majority, representing 41%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Missing Data This section will contain information related to missingness in the NCRP data. Keep?


# Prison Population ## Prison Population Trends From 2010 to 2022, the prison population decreased 39%, changing from 165,062 in 2010 to 101,441 in 2022. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners Series
## Demographics ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners 2022 - Race and Ethnicity

``` ::: :::
## Sentence Lengths ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Prison Releases ## Prison Release Trends From 2010 to 2020, prison releases decreased 64%, dropping from 126,694 in 2010 to 45,194 in 2020. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
In 2020, 58% of people eligible for parole were released during their eligibility year. This represents a 58% increase compared to 2010. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Conditional Releases Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Demographics of People Released The demographics of people released from prison reveal notable proportions among Hispanic, any race and white, non-hispanic people. Gender distribution indicates a predominance of males over females. Age-wise, the majority of people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. These findings provide insights into the populations transitioning back into the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Time Served by Offense ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Disparities ## Incarceration Rates In California, Black, non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 10.5 times higher and Hispanic, any race people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 2.4 times higher than White, non-Hispanic people, when accounting for population sizes in the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Years Spent in Prison After Parole Eligibility In 2020, people in prison past their parole eligibility year collectively spent a total of 2,493 years behind bars instead of supervised in the community on parole. This extended incarceration varied across different racial and ethnic groups.

## Timing of Release After Parole Eligibility The timing of release for individuals after their parole eligibility year varied by offense type and demographic group, with many remaining in prison for several years beyond their eligibility date. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Time Served ::: {.cell} ::: Black, non-Hispanic individuals faced the longest average time served in prison in 2020, with an average of 4.4 years. Hispanic, any race individuals experienced shorter prison stays, averaging 3 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: By offense type, disparities were observed in time served by race and ethnicity. For Rape or Sexual Assault offenses, Black, non-Hispanic individuals had 2.71 more years on average compared to Hispanic, any race individuals, who had the shortest time served for these offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
